attacks.name: Sonicboom
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Baton Pass
attacks.effect: You may switch Yanma with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, move as many Energy cards attached to Yanma as you like to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It can hover in one spot by flapping its wings at high speed. It flits about to guard its territory.

attacks.name: Deep Sea Pressure
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost is ColorlessColorless more.

attacks.name: Aqua Wave
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: A rare Pokémon discovered during a deep-sea exploration. It has not changed in over 100 million years.

attacks.name: Dwindling Wave
attacks.effect: Does 80 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Milotic.

flavorText: Its lovely scales are described as rainbow colored. They change color depending on the viewing angle.

attacks.name: Lightning Burn
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to be present wherever there is a lightning storm.

attacks.name: Hyper Flame
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a Fire Energy attached to Moltres. If tails, discard all Energy attached to Moltres.

flavorText: The flames on this legendary Pokémon's wings burn so brightly that they can make night seem like day.

attacks.name: Diamond Dust
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to freeze the water in the air during winter, causing snow.

attacks.name: Defog
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, you may discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, this attack's base damage is 70 instead of 40.

rules: Put this card onto your Active Staraptor FB. Staraptor FB LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

attacks.name: Final Blowup
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy attached to Rayquaza C. Ignore this effect if you have no cards in your hand.

rules: Put this card onto your Active Rayquaza C. Rayquaza C LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

attacks.name: Dragon Rush
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy attached to Garchomp C. Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Garchomp C can't use Dragon Rush during your next turn.

rules: Put this card onto your Active Garchomp C. Garchomp C LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

attacks.name: Powerful Spark
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

rules: Put this card onto your Active Electivire FB. Electivire FB LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

attacks.name: Malevolent Fire
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Energy attached to Charizard G.

rules: Put this card onto your Active Charizard G. Charizard G LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

attacks.name: Jet Shoot
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Blaziken FB by attacks is increased by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Put this card onto your Active Blaziken FB. Blaziken FB LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

attacks.name: Darkness Slugger
attacks.effect: You may discard a card from your hand. If you do, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

rules: Put this card onto your Active Absol G. Absol G LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.
Search your discard pile for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your discard pile for up to 5 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, put all cards in your hand on top of your deck. Then, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Flip 2 coins. If either of them is heads, look at your opponent's hand. For each heads, choose 1 card from your opponent's hand and put it on the bottom of your opponent's deck in any order.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

The Retreat cost for each Pokémon SP (both yours and your opponent's) is Colorless less.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Whenever any player plays any Pokémon from his or her hand to Level-Up 1 of his or her Pokémon, remove 4 damage counters from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Quick Turn
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It checks its surroundings and location using reflections of the ultrasonic waves from its mouth.

attacks.name: Tone-Deaf
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Usually, its cries are like quiet murmurs. If frightened, it shrieks at the same volume as a jet plane.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: The shell on its back is made of soil. On a very healthy Turtwig, the shell should feel moist.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.
Because they are weak individually, they form groups. However, they bicker if the group grows too big.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Tail Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: As soon as the tail claws close, its needle tips secrete poison. It can survive a year without food.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.
The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: A Pokémon that lives on icebergs. It swims in the sea using the point on its head to break up ice.
To protect itself from attackers, it curls up into a ball. It lives in arid regions with minimal rainfall.

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The more healthy the Roselia, the more pleasant its flowers' aroma. Its scent deeply relaxes people.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its powerful tackles can destroy anything. However, it is too slow witted to help people work.
A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host Paras.

attacks.name: Pachi
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Dash Attack
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It grows underground, sensing its surroundings using antennae instead of its virtually blind eyes.

attacks.name: Mud Sport
attacks.effect: If Mudkip has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

attacks.name: Encore
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can use only that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It mimics the expressions and motions of those it sees to understand the feelings of others.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is nocturnal in nature. If it spots something shiny, its eyes glitter brightly.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Meditite.

flavorText: It always trains deep in mountains. It levitates when it heightens its spiritual power through meditation.

attacks.name: Extra Punch
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon LV.X, this attack does 10 damage plus 50 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: The faster the units at its sides rotate, the greater the magnetic force they generate.

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A Magikarp living for many years can leap a mountain using Splash. The move remains useless, though.
Its legs are short. Whenever it stumbles, its stiff antennae clack with a xylophone-like sound.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Growlithe.

flavorText: A Pokémon with a loyal nature. It will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Goldeen.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Though it appears very elegant when swimming with fins unfurled, it can jab powerfully with its horn.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It attacks using its huge mouth. While its attacks are powerful, it hurts itself out of clumsiness, too.

attacks.name: Focus Fist
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: At rest, it looks just like a rock. Carelessly stepping on it will make it swing its fists angrily.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Count and Draw
attacks.effect: Draw a card for each of your opponent's Pokémon that isn't an Evolved Pokémon.

flavorText: It is a shabby and ugly Pokémon. However, it is very hardy and can survive on little water.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: Drifloon does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a "Signpost for Wandering Spirits."

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: Doduo does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.
It rarely fights fairly, but that is strictly to ensure survival. It is popular as a mascot.

attacks.name: Sharp Pincers
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: Its hardy vitality enables it to adapt to any environment. Its pincers will never release prey.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Remove 2 damage counters from Combee.

flavorText: The trio is together from birth. It constantly gathers honey from flowers to please Vespiquen.

attacks.name: Chime
attacks.effect: Search your opponent's discard pile for a Supporter card and use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack. (The Supporter card remains in your opponent's discard pile.)

flavorText: There is an orb inside its mouth. When it hops, the orb bounces all over and makes a ringing sound.

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It is very agile. Before going to sleep, it extinguishes the flame on its tail to prevent fires.

attacks.name: Growth
attacks.effect: Attach a Grass Energy from your hand to Cherubi.

flavorText: The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Nosedive
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Chatot does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its tongue is just like a human's. As a result, it can cleverly mimic human speech.

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.effect: Switch Buneary with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mini Drain
attacks.effect: Remove 1 damage counter from Buneary.

flavorText: Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.
For some time after its birth, it grows by gaining nourishment from the seed on its back.
It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: A comparison revealed that Bidoof's front teeth grow at the same rate as Rattata's.

attacks.name: Pit Search
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It converses with others by using magnetic pulses. In a swarm, they move in perfect unison.

attacks.name: Quick Turn
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It moves by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to Yanma.

flavorText: It can hover is one spot by flapping its wings at high speed. It flits about to guard its territory.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Wailmer but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: Wailmer does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: On sunny days, it lands on beaches to bounce like a ball and play. It spouts water from its nose.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: Togekiss C does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Double Peck
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Air Crash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Lock Up
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Silver Feather
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, when your opponent puts a Basic Pokémon from his or her hand onto his or her Bench, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Max
attacks.effect: Discard all Metal Energy attached to Skarmory FB. Flip a coin for each Energy card you discarded. This attack does 20 damage plus 40 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.effect: Discard all Lightning Energy attached to Rotom. Flip a coin for each Energy card you discarded. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Uproar
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its electric-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

attacks.name: Poison Seed
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Each of its hands contains different toxins, but both hands can jab with near-fatal power.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Standing on its hind legs freed its forelegs and made it smarter. It is very forgetful, however.

attacks.name: Grand Swell
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon for each Pokémon Tool and Stadium card your opponent has in play. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: A rare Pokémon discovered during a deep-sea exploration. It has not changed in over 100 million years.

attacks.name: Find
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for a Trainer card or a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

attacks.name: Biting Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin, if heads this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Gigashock
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

attacks.name: Greedy Draw
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards or less in your hand as your opponent, draw cards until you have 1 more card than your opponent. (If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Hand Charge
attacks.effect: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

attacks.name: Gripthrow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent returns the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to his or her hand.

attacks.name: Sever
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: It grips prey with its pincers until the prey is torn in half. What it can't tear, it tosses far.

attacks.name: Draw In
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Fire Energy cards and attach them to Numel.

flavorText: Its humped back stores intensely hot magma. In rain, the magma cools, slowing its movement.

attacks.name: Circling Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch Ninjask with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Parallel Drain
attacks.effect: Remove from 1 of your Pokémon the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Because it moves so quickly, it sometimes becomes unseeable. It congregates around tree sap.

attacks.name: Switcheroo
attacks.effect: Move a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon (excluding Pokémon that already has a Pokémon Tool attached to it). (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing.)

flavorText: If spotted, it will lure an unwary person into chasing it, then lose the pursuer on mountain trails.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Trash Charge
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

attacks.name: Cleansing Ring
attacks.effect: You may discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, remove 4 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scale Blow
attacks.effect: Does 90 damage minus 10 damage for each card in your hand.

flavorText: Its lovely scales are described as rainbow colored. They change color depending on the viewing angle.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its steel body won't be scratched if it collides with a jet.

attacks.name: Skim Attack
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.
Its sturdy legs give it sure footing, even in mud. It burrows into dirt to sleep.

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to Manectric G.

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Magmar.

flavorText: When it breathes deeply, heat waves form around its body, making it hard to see clearly.

attacks.name: Stomp Off
attacks.effect: Discard the top card from your opponent's deck.

flavorText: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.
When the bud on its back starts swelling, a sweet aroma wafts to indicate the flower's coming bloom.

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It shuts its nostrils tight then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around ten.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Healing Scale
attacks.effect: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It loves sparkly things. It seeks treasures in caves and hoards the loot in its nest.

attacks.name: Jet Screw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It is a common sight around fishing ports. It is known to rescue people and carry off prey.
At dusk, swarms of them are carried aloft on winds. When noticed, they suddenly vanish.

attacks.name: Mach Blow
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon SP, this attack's base damage is 80 instead of 20.

attacks.name: Giant Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. Cherrim is now Confused.

flavorText: If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun's rays with its whole body.

attacks.name: Search and Escape
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Put Chatot G and all cards attached to it on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Swallow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, remove from Carnivine the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

attacks.name: Hover Hit
attacks.effect: The Retreat Cost for the Defending Pokémon is 0 until the end of your next turn.

attacks.name: Crash Bomber
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Special Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage. Then, discard a Special Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A river dammed by Bibarel will never overflow its banks, which is appreciated by people nearby.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Sonic Wing
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Lightning Wing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.effect: Remove all Special Conditions and 4 damage counters from Wailord. Wailord is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Giant Wave
attacks.effect: Wailord can't use Giant Wave during your next turn.

flavorText: The biggest of all Pokémon. It can dive to a depth of almost 10,000 feet on only one breath.

attacks.name: Synchro Removal
attacks.effect: If any Energy card attached to Spinda is the same type as any Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon, discard 1 of those Energy cards from the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pulled Punch
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack's base damage is 10 instead of 30.

flavorText: No two Spinda have the same pattern of spots. Its tottering step fouls the aim of foes.

attacks.name: Luna Turn
attacks.effect: If Lunatone is in your discard pile, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage. Then, search your discard pile for Lunatone, show it to your opponent, and shuffle it into your deck.

flavorText: A new Pokémon species, rumored to be from the sun. It gives off light while spinning.

attacks.name: Spike Wound
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A discarded bug shell that came to life. Peering into the crack on its back is said to steal one's spirit.

attacks.name: Horn Pierce
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Endure
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, if Seaking would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, Seaking is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

flavorText: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

attacks.name: Needle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

flavorText: It curls up, then rolls into foes with its back. Its sharp spines inflict severe damage.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.effect: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and remove 2 damage counters from Roserade C.

attacks.name: Top Drop
attacks.effect: Discard the top card from your opponent's deck. If you discarded a Pokémon, this attack does damage equal to the HP of that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brick Break
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It grows angry if you see its eyes and gets angrier if you run. If you beat it, it gets even madder.

attacks.name: Nutritional Support
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Grass Energy cards and attach them to any of your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hibernation Spore
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, the Defending Pokémon is still Asleep.

flavorText: A mushroom grown larger than the host's body controls Parasect. It scatters poisonous spores.

attacks.name: Desperate Slap
attacks.effect: If Mr. Mime already has 5 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.

flavorText: It shapes an invisible wall in midair by minutely vibrating its fingertips to stop molecules in the air.

attacks.name: Sky Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

attacks.name: Aqua Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Water Energy attached to Milotic C. This attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Channeling
attacks.effect: Your opponent draws 2 cards.

attacks.name: Chakra Points
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It gains the ability to see the aura of its opponents by honing its mind through starvation.

attacks.name: Turnaround Standby
attacks.effect: During your next turn, any damage done by Mawile's Swallow or Bite to your opponent's Active Pokémon is increased by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Swallow
attacks.effect: Remove from Mawile the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gravity Wave
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that doesn't have a Retreat Cost. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Because it turns active on nights of the full moon, it is said to have some link to the lunar phases.

attacks.name: Mid-air Strike
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Licking-Licking Heal
attacks.effect: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, remove 2 damage counters from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Return
attacks.effect: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Riot
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Pokémon that isn't an evolved Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: If one utters a deep cry, many Murkrow gather quickly. For this, it is called "Summoner of Night."

attacks.name: Knock Back
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rushing Water
attacks.effect: Move an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Escort
attacks.effect: If you played any Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Ghost Hand
attacks.effect: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cursed Wrath
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon SP in your discard pile.

flavorText: When Doduo evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.

attacks.name: Ice Shard
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Fighting Pokémon, this attack's base damage is 80 instead of 30.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.effect: You may switch Crawdaunt G with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Antique Magic
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on any Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) in any way you like.

attacks.name: Synchro Attack
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has the same remaining HP as Claydol, this attack's base damage is 90 instead of 30.

flavorText: An ancient clay figurine that came to life as a Pokémon from exposure to a mysterious ray of light.

attacks.name: Heal Bell
attacks.effect: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Super Psywave
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Count the amount of Energy attached to that Pokémon. Put that many damage counters on the Pokémon.

flavorText: Its cries echo inside its hollow body to emerge as beautiful notes for startling and repelling foes.

attacks.name: Flame Jet
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Earth Power
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Moving Fire
attacks.effect: You may move a Fire Energy card attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to Camerupt.

attacks.name: Volcanic Crash
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. For each tails, discard the top card of your deck. Ignore this effect if your opponent has any Water Pokémon in play.

flavorText: It has volcanoes on its back. If magma builds up in its body, it shudders, then erupts violently.

attacks.name: Select Powder
attacks.effect: Choose either Burned or Poisoned. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

attacks.name: Sharp Beak
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Wind Return
attacks.effect: You may return all Grass Energy attached to Yanmega to your hand. If you do, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card you returned.

attacks.name: Desperate Pollen
attacks.effect: If Venusaur already has 8 or more damage counters on it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Special Reaction
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by any Special Conditions, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage for each of those Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, you may switch your opponent's Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Push Over
attacks.effect: Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each Fighting Energy attached to Swampert.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Raging Drill
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Rhyperior.

attacks.name: Deep Scrap
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon would be Knocked Out by this attack, discard the top 3 cards from your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It can launch a rock held in its hand like a missile by tightening then expanding muscles instantly.

attacks.name: Drain Punch
attacks.effect: Remove from Regigigas FB a number of damage counters equal to the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rainbow Lariat
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the number of different types of Pokémon SP you have in play.

attacks.name: Trash Burst
attacks.effect: You may discard up to 5 Energy cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card you discarded.

attacks.name: Twister
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both are tails, this attack does nothing. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Geo Impact
attacks.effect: If you have a Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that is the same type as the Defending Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Metang combined to form it. With four brains, it has the intelligence of a supercomputer.

attacks.name: Fire Arrow
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Flame Ball
attacks.effect: You may move a Fire Energy card attached to Magmortar to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Guard Claw
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Garchomp by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Speed Impact
attacks.effect: Does 120 damage minus 20 damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dump and Draw
attacks.effect: Discard up to 2 Energy cards from your hand. For each card you discarded, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Electric Current
attacks.effect: Move a Lightning Energy card attached to Electivire FB to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Ball
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. Apply Weakness and Resistance.

attacks.name: Luring Flame
attacks.effect: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Vapor Kick
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Water Pokémon in play, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Doom News
attacks.effect: Return all Energy cards attached to Absol G to your hand. The Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out at the end of your opponent's next turn.
